verses.latin_vulgate: Quid videbis in Sulamite, nisi choros castrorum ?     Quam pulchri sunt gressus tui in calceamentis, filia principis !  Juncturæ femorum tuorum sicut monilia  quæ fabricata sunt manu artificis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־יָּפ֧וּ פְעָמַ֛יִךְ בַּנְּעָלִ֖ים בַּת־נָדִ֑יב חַמּוּקֵ֣י יְרֵכַ֔יִךְ כְּמ֣וֹ חֲלָאִ֔ים מַעֲשֵׂ֖ה יְדֵ֥י אׇמָּֽן׃
verses.king_james: How beautiful are thy feet with shoes, O prince’s daughter! the joints of thy thighs are like jewels, the work of the hands of a cunning workman.

verses.latin_vulgate: Umbilicus tuus crater tornatilis,  numquam indigens poculis.  Venter tuus sicut acervus tritici vallatus liliis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שׇׁרְרֵךְ֙ אַגַּ֣ן הַסַּ֔הַר אַל־יֶחְסַ֖ר הַמָּ֑זֶג בִּטְנֵךְ֙ עֲרֵמַ֣ת חִטִּ֔ים סוּגָ֖ה בַּשּׁוֹשַׁנִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: Thy navel is like a round goblet, which wanteth not liquor: thy belly is like an heap of wheat set about with lilies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Duo ubera tua sicut duo hinnuli,  gemelli capreæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁנֵ֥י שָׁדַ֛יִךְ כִּשְׁנֵ֥י עֳפָרִ֖ים תׇּאֳמֵ֥י צְבִיָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Thy two breasts are like two young roes that are twins.

verses.latin_vulgate: Collum tuum sicut turris eburnea ;  oculi tui sicut piscinæ in Hesebon  quæ sunt in porta filiæ multitudinis.  Nasus tuus sicut turris Libani,  quæ respicit contra Damascum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צַוָּארֵ֖ךְ כְּמִגְדַּ֣ל הַשֵּׁ֑ן עֵינַ֜יִךְ בְּרֵכ֣וֹת בְּחֶשְׁבּ֗וֹן עַל־שַׁ֙עַר֙ בַּת־רַבִּ֔ים אַפֵּךְ֙ כְּמִגְדַּ֣ל הַלְּבָנ֔וֹן צוֹפֶ֖ה פְּנֵ֥י דַמָּֽשֶׂק׃
verses.king_james: Thy neck is as a tower of ivory; thine eyes like the fishpools in Heshbon, by the gate of Bath–rabbim: thy nose is as the tower of Lebanon which looketh toward Damascus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Caput tuum ut Carmelus ;  et comæ capitis tui sicut purpura regis  vincta canalibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֹאשֵׁ֤ךְ עָלַ֙יִךְ֙ כַּכַּרְמֶ֔ל וְדַלַּ֥ת רֹאשֵׁ֖ךְ כָּאַרְגָּמָ֑ן מֶ֖לֶךְ אָס֥וּר בָּרְהָטִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Thine head upon thee is like Carmel, and the hair of thine head like purple; the king is held in the galleries.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam pulchra es, et quam decora,  carissima, in deliciis !
verses.king_james: How fair and how pleasant art thou, O love, for delights!

verses.latin_vulgate: Statura tua assimilata est palmæ,  et ubera tua botris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֹ֤את קֽוֹמָתֵךְ֙ דָּֽמְתָ֣ה לְתָמָ֔ר וְשָׁדַ֖יִךְ לְאַשְׁכֹּלֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: This thy stature is like to a palm tree, and thy breasts to clusters of grapes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixi : Ascendam in palmam,  et apprehendam fructus ejus ;  et erunt ubera tua sicut botri vineæ,  et odor oris tui sicut malorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָמַ֙רְתִּי֙ אֶעֱלֶ֣ה בְתָמָ֔ר אֹֽחֲזָ֖ה בְּסַנְסִנָּ֑יו וְיִֽהְיוּ־נָ֤א שָׁדַ֙יִךְ֙ כְּאֶשְׁכְּל֣וֹת הַגֶּ֔פֶן וְרֵ֥יחַ אַפֵּ֖ךְ כַּתַּפּוּחִֽים׃
verses.king_james: I said, I will go up to the palm tree, I will take hold of the boughs thereof: now also thy breasts shall be as clusters of the vine, and the smell of thy nose like apples;

verses.latin_vulgate: Guttur tuum sicut vinum optimum,  dignum dilecto meo ad potandum,  labiisque et dentibus illius ad ruminandum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְחִכֵּ֕ךְ כְּיֵ֥ין הַטּ֛וֹב הוֹלֵ֥ךְ לְדוֹדִ֖י לְמֵישָׁרִ֑ים דּוֹבֵ֖ב שִׂפְתֵ֥י יְשֵׁנִֽים׃
verses.king_james: And the roof of thy mouth like the best wine for my beloved, that goeth down sweetly, causing the lips of those that are asleep to speak.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego dilecto meo,  et ad me conversio ejus.
verses.king_james: I am my beloved’s, and his desire is toward me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Veni, dilecte mi, egrediamur in agrum,  commoremur in villis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְכָ֤ה דוֹדִי֙ נֵצֵ֣א הַשָּׂדֶ֔ה נָלִ֖ינָה בַּכְּפָרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Come, my beloved, let us go forth into the field; let us lodge in the villages.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mane surgamus ad vineas :  videamus si floruit vinea,  si flores fructus parturiunt,  si floruerunt mala punica ;  ibi dabo tibi ubera mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַשְׁכִּ֙ימָה֙ לַכְּרָמִ֔ים נִרְאֶ֞ה אִם־פָּֽרְחָ֤ה הַגֶּ֙פֶן֙ פִּתַּ֣ח הַסְּמָדַ֔ר הֵנֵ֖צוּ הָרִמּוֹנִ֑ים שָׁ֛ם אֶתֵּ֥ן אֶת־דֹּדַ֖י לָֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Let us get up early to the vineyards; let us see if the vine flourish, whether the tender grape appear, and the pomegranates bud forth: there will I give thee my loves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mandragoræ dederunt odorem  in portis nostris omnia poma :  nova et vetera, dilecte mi, servavi tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַֽדּוּדָאִ֣ים נָֽתְנוּ־רֵ֗יחַ וְעַל־פְּתָחֵ֙ינוּ֙ כׇּל־מְגָדִ֔ים חֲדָשִׁ֖ים גַּם־יְשָׁנִ֑ים דּוֹדִ֖י צָפַ֥נְתִּי לָֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: The mandrakes give a smell, and at our gates are all manner of pleasant fruits, new and old, which I have laid up for thee, O my beloved.
